justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and thoroughly (Customer Success competitive differentiation vs Operations driver welfare/retention). Formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction' explicitly stating executive arbitration is required. No acceptance criteria produced (empty array). Confidence level is 35, well under the 45 threshold. Option C is proposed as a compromise but clearly labeled as a recommendation requiring 'executive sign-off and Legal review before any engineering work begins' — not presented as a resolution.

observations.text: Escalation is exceptionally well-structured with explicit options (A-D), affected stakeholders, and a clear recommendation that defers final authority to executive leadership
business_rules and acceptance_criteria arrays are empty, correctly reflecting that no invariants can be finalized until the conflict is resolved
Identified a third stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) that neither team consulted, adding a second escalation for mandatory privacy impact assessment
rejection_reasons clearly articulate why the spec is unbuildable as stated
Edge cases are insightful and illustrate real-world consequences of exact GPS sharing without taking sides
Confidence reasoning is thorough — explains why confidence is low and distinguishes high-confidence areas (the conflict is real) from low-confidence areas (which side should win)
Industry precedent cited (Uber, DoorDash, Amazon) to ground the compromise proposal in reality rather than speculation

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands zero location sharing due to driver retention problems. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'missing_context'
